description: Auto Layout constraint DSL for UIKit and AppKit, similar to SnapKit and Anchorage
rules: Supports iOS 15+, tvOS 15+, macOS 12+. No watchOS, no SwiftUI.
Use the OrbitalView typealias for cross-platform code: it resolves to UIView on UIKit and NSView on AppKit.
Main entry point is the .orbital proxy on any view, e.g. view.orbital.layout(.top(16), .leading(16), .width(120)).
layout() creates and activates constraints; prepareLayout() creates them without activating; update() and remake() modify existing ones.
Trailing, bottom, and right constants are auto-negated — always pass positive insets like .trailing(16), not -16.
Use orbit(child:) to addSubview and set translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false in one call.
firstBaseline and lastBaseline anchors are UIKit-only and guarded at compile time — do not use them in macOS code.
OrbitalProxy, OrbitalDescriptor, ConstraintStorage, and ConstraintFactory are @MainActor — call them from the main actor.
